hero.question: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
hero.prob_take: The crowd is pricing a 62.5% chance of U.S. military action against Iran by 2027, which assumes sustained escalation despite the region's tendency toward brinkmanship without full-scale conflict. The real tell is the 3-point drop in 24 hours, suggesting smart money is selling into rhetoric and taking the under because every actual Iran crisis of the last 20 years stopped short of invasion.

daily_take.headline: Iran Invasion Odds Crack. The Market Just Signaled Something.
daily_take.deck: A 3-point drop in 24 hours on a $1.3M market screams repositioning, not noise. The 62.5% price assumes a 2027 invasion is almost certain. It's not. Smart money is pricing out the near-term shock scenario and betting on diplomatic theater instead. Short the fear.

sidebar.headline: March deadline is the real tell. 18.5% odds suggest the market knows early escalation stays unlikely.
sidebar.label: Iran Invasion by March 31: 18.5%

sidebar.headline: Mavericks catching real momentum. Up 5 points in a day signals sharp action found value.
sidebar.label: Mavericks vs. Bucks: 31.0%

sidebar.headline: Nuclear deal at 15.5% with nearly zero volume is the market's way of saying nobody believes it happens this year.
sidebar.label: US-Iran Nuclear Deal: 15.5%
